LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER A DANGEROUS CONDITION EXISTED AND WHETHER DEFENDANT CREATED AND/OR HAD ACTUAL AND/OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF THE ALLEGED DEFECTS. A REASONABLE TRIER OF FACT COULD CONCLUDE THAT THE CONFIGURATION OF THE STREET AND THE ABSENCE OF A PEDESTRIAN PATHWAY CONSTITUTES A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. AS TO CONSTRUCTIVE/ACTUAL NOTICE, DEFENDANT CREATED THE "TWIN PEAKS PEDESTRIAN & BIKEWAY IMPROVEMENTS" PLAN BECAUSE IT WAS AWARE OF THE DANGER, BUT NEVER COMPLETED ANY SAFETY CHANGES. (HOM DEPO., 42:7-9; 69:2-9; 69:20-70:14). THE EVIDENCE ALSO SHOWS THAT CITIZENS MADE COMPLAINTS TO DEFENDANT REGARDING THE CONDITION OF THE PROPERTY. (YEN DEC., EX. M). GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 830.4 AND 830.8 DO NOT BAR THIS ACTION BECAUSE THE LACK OF SIGNAGE/SIGNALS IS MERELY ONE COMPONENT OF THE ASSERTED DANGEROUS CONDITION OF THE PUBLIC PROPERTY. IN ADDITION, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE CONDITION WAS A TRAP. MS. EUNICE'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS ADEQUATELY PLED PER CCP 452. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ANDREW P. MCDEVITT, ESQ. of Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Schoenberger, Tel No. 415-981-7210 appearing for Plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are triable issues whether a dangerous condition existed and whether defendant created and/or had actual and/or constructive notice of the alleged defects. A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that the configuration of the street and the absence of a pedestrian pathway constitutes a dangerous condition of public property. As to constructive/actual notice, Defendant created the "Twin Peaks Pedestrian & Bikeway Improvements" plan because it was aware of the danger, but never completed any safety changes. (Hom Depo., 42:7-9; 69:2-9; 69:20-70:14). The evidence also shows that citizens made complaints to Defendant regarding the condition of the property. (Yen Dec., Ex. M). Government Code sections 830.4 and 830.8 do not bar this action because the lack of signage/signals is merely one component of the asserted dangerous condition of the public property. In addition, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the condition was a trap. Ms. Eunice's cross-complaint is adequately pled per CCP 452. COURT SIGNS IN OPEN COURT THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY THE CARTWRIGHT LAW FIRM, INC. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
